calibration.trend_state_question: No transitions warranted; no new flags. The Day-105 T3 CONTESTED flag and T4 strain already STOOD DOWN at the v4.4 increment (Day 109) on second-cycle non-fire; nothing this cycle reverses that. The T8 advance_watch reading-refinement (the spoiler is a 60-day-window risk via Lebanon, not a signature-moment strike on Iran) is CONFIRMED this cycle, which is the opposite of a reading-swing: the operational reading asserted Day 109 was vindicated within one cycle when the Lebanon-axis spoiler went live exactly as flagged. No VALIDATED trend contradicted on single-cycle or multi-cycle evidence. T2 advances (reserved-channel deterrent leverage); T4 advances (US-exec-vs-Israeli-maximalist divergence at its sharpest). No /audit flag generated.
calibration.thesis_question: HOLDING, reinforced. The v4.4 central thesis and the §5.30 post-signature collision-class architecture both predicted the Lebanon carve-out as the live residual/snap-back surface (Israel explicitly reserved freedom of action; ceasefire 'all fronts' collides with Katz 'not bound'). One cycle after signature the residual is materializing precisely there: Iran (Khatam al-Anbiya + FM) calls continued Israeli Lebanon operations a violation of the MoU and threatens a hard response; Netanyahu defies Trump and commits to occupying south Lebanon; Trump pressures Netanyahu publicly. The framework ranked the deferral and named the dominant spoiler vector; the actors are selecting within that surface (Netanyahu continues Lebanon ops, Iran reserves the Hezbollah channel, Trump applies principal-level pressure). This is confirmation of the deferral-with-Lebanon-residual structure, not drift. The signature itself holds (digital, named signatories, ceremony pending); the analytical problem has relocated into the 60-day window as predicted.

ABC: US intel pessimistic on Iran's willingness to make nuclear concessions reinforces the stall path

finding_cards.target: Israeli internal politics / operational posture (BS-3) — pre-emption signaling, Trump-Netanyahu divergence, Lebanon axis, Knesset dissolution
sources_consulted: Al Jazeera (Trump 'more responsible' / Netanyahu south Lebanon)
discounts_applied: Trump verbal criticism scored on the documented action context (public principal-level rebuke at the G7, multi-outlet T2), not as near-zero rhetoric, because it is corroborated and is itself the data point (a US-exec posture toward an ally)
finding_cards.finding: The Israeli-pre-emption spoiler materialized through the Lebanon axis exactly as the v4.4 §5.30 / Day-109 reading-refinement predicted. Netanyahu defied Trump's demand to halt Hezbollah operations, said Israeli troops will occupy south Lebanon, and held that 'Trump's agreement does not bind us'; Katz fixed the IDF in the security zones. Trump rebuked Netanyahu publicly (June 16): he wants him 'more responsible with respect to Lebanon', is 'not happy', reportedly told aides he was 'p---ed off', and warned the renewed fighting 'throws a negative light on the big deal'. This is the sharpest documented Trump-Netanyahu principal-level divergence of the conflict. Knesset context: the dissolution bill cleared its preliminary reading (110-0; first reading 106-0 June 2) over the haredi-draft crisis; election now movable to September / mid-October (forward from Oct 27), preserving Netanyahu's pace control. No Israeli strike on Iran this cycle.
finding_cards.trigger_match: partial / next-cycle. No Iran-nuclear-site strike (P85-02 unfired); but the Lebanon-axis spoiler is now LIVE, repricing Fork D'-v up and re-arming Fork C. P102-02 (IDF Lebanon escalation drawing a direct Iranian response) is firing-adjacent

finding_cards.proposed_revision: 30d: shift mass from Fork D'-i (36-48% -> 32-44%) to D'-v (12-20% -> 16-24%); Fork C 12-20% -> 14-22%. No architecture change (the vector is inside v4.4 §5.30). SITREP repaces; no /revise

finding_cards.conflict_notes: A2 (Netanyahu Principal-Access Channel) PROVISIONAL, cycle 3 of the ~6-cycle window (demote ~Day 123). Step 7b.5 forcing-question restate: the discriminating signal that resolves the candidate set is a White House readout claiming 'full dismantlement / all HEU removed' that the signed text does not contain (= Penetration-relay validated) vs the executive openly diverging from Netanyahu on Lebanon and tolerating a deal that defers HEU (= relay inherited, demote). THIS CYCLE CUTS HARD AGAINST A2: Trump publicly rebuking Netanyahu while defending a deal Netanyahu resists is the opposite of a penetrated executive relaying Israeli maximalism. Supports demotion at the window close absent a contradicting readout.

finding_cards.finding: The signature holds and the formal ceremony is set for FRIDAY JUNE 19 in Geneva/Switzerland (multi-outlet T2: CSIS, CNBC, CBS, NPR). NOTE / OPERATIONAL CORRECTION: the Day-109 SITREP recorded the ceremony as 'June 20'; June 19 is the Friday and the consensus T2 date — correct forward to June 19. The MoU is a broad framework 'about a page and a half', leaving terms to the 60-day technical talks. Live friction this cycle is interpretive ('differing versions'): the frozen-assets figure and conditionality diverge across Iranian and US framings, and Iran insists the MoU binds Israel on Lebanon while the US and Israel dispute that. Iran will not begin implementation until the June-19 signing. CSIS places the agreement probability at 80-85%. No E3 role; IAEA slated for the nuclear talks. HEU 440.9kg 60% remains in Tehran, deferred to the window (ISIS/IAEA: near-total loss of monitoring; possible transfer to Isfahan).
finding_cards.trigger_match: yes (interpretive). P93-01 already fired (Day 109); this cycle is durability + the interpretation dispute. No new fork-activation trigger; the 'differing versions' + Lebanon-binding dispute feed Fork D'-v
finding_cards.direction: no change (composite); supports the D'-i -> D'-v internal shift
finding_cards.proposed_revision: Operational: correct the ceremony date to Friday June 19. Matrix: the interpretation dispute (frozen assets; Lebanon-binding clause) is a D'-v input, folded into the PROBE-9 repricing. No standalone matrix move
reference_trends: T1 hold — Pakistan/Qatar/Oman mediator stack intact; G7 channel persists
T3 hold — mid-tier Ghalibaf signature; apex unowned
finding_cards.conflict_notes: The 'differing versions' dispute is the two-level game made textual: each side projects domestically favorable terms onto a deliberately thin instrument. This is consistent with T3 (apex reserves the floor; mid-tier negotiates the range) and does not by itself contradict the signature. Discriminating signal next cycle: whether the June-19 ceremony produces a single agreed text or surfaces the divergences as a ratification break.

finding_cards.target: Energy market structural breaks (BS-7) — Brent, Hormuz status, blockade, war-risk pricing
discounts_applied: Intraday Brent prints diverge across sources ($81.55 spot / $82.97 futures -0.24% / one source $78.82 -5.23%); reported as a range, not averaged, with the consensus that the deal-pricing decline continues
finding_cards.finding: Brent extended the deal-pricing decline on June 16, trading roughly $81.55-83 (consensus continued softening from the Day-109 $83.17; one source as low as $78.82), after the steepest one-day drop in months. Drivers: the signed agreement, lifted blockade, and 30-day Hormuz-reopening commitment. The deal makes Hormuz 'permanently toll free' — a NOTABLE TERM-LEVEL REVERSAL of the prior Iran-Oman 'permanent toll' framing the framework tracked (Iran conceded the toll claim in the signed text). The 107-day blockade ended; at least five Iranian vessels (three oil tankers, two cargo) transited after the lift. Downside floor questioned: ~20+ uncleared naval mines, idled-field restart, and damaged-facility repair cap the decline; Pentagon assesses mine-clearing at up to six months at accelerated pace.
finding_cards.trigger_match: yes — confirms the deal-pricing repricing; no re-escalation signal. Reinforces the D'-i operative read and the de-escalation rotation
finding_cards.proposed_revision: No standalone matrix move; Brent direction is a coincident confirmer. Watch the asymmetric reversal trigger: a close back above $92 on a ceremony failure (P108-04) would confirm snap-back

finding_cards.finding: Media now frames Mojtaba Khamenei explicitly as the seated Supreme Leader ('decisions require approval from the supreme leader, now Mojtaba Khamenei'), the deal debate having reignited a power-structure argument. Hardliner protests (Saturday, Tehran + Mashhad) targeted Ghalibaf and Araghchi; the chant 'Ghalibaf, Araghchi — what about the blood of our leader?' referenced the assassination of the previous Supreme Leader earlier in 2026. Kayhan's Shariatmadari attacked the framework as surrendering a bargaining chip and cited the leader's compensation demand. CNN assessed 'the regime is likely to have the final say'. CRITICALLY: no Mojtaba-direct authenticated statement owning or repudiating the signed MoU — the apex still has not publicly owned the terms; the mid-tier (Ghalibaf) signed.

The media 'Mojtaba is Supreme Leader' framing is inherited, not validated: it does not establish that Mojtaba (vs the IRGC council / functional command) made the deal decision; the delegated-signing modality (Ghalibaf signs, apex silent, hardliner dissent managed indirectly) continues to point toward the diffuse/delegated end. Carried at M; demote toward structurally-opaque at the window close absent discriminating evidence.

Nominal: no new operation name; blockade lifted; ceasefire all fronts; a CENTCOM Bahrain maritime-coordination mechanism is restoring commercial navigation. Operational: at least five Iranian vessels transited after the lift; ~20+ uncleared mines remain (up to six months to clear); zero new US KIA; no transit-enforcement action against a US vessel. Both the nominal and operational axes read de-escalation; no 'pressure-while-talking' middle-register signal this cycle.
